title: hieracium murorum subsp. morulum
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. morulum, also known as Wall Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can be attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing pain, inflammation, and fever, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. Hieracium murorum subsp. morulum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in a variety of traditional remedies.
